pageTimings.text:
It was the middle of the night.
Everyone was nearly asleep.
Then a light flashed.
Then another.
Then small glowing things began to fall from the sky.

The small things landed and bounced.
They bounced along the ground.
They made a sound like giggling.
They left gold trails where they bounced.
"What ARE they?" said Pip.

Pip caught one.
It was warm.
It had very large golden eyes.
It looked up at Pip and giggled.

"What are you?" asked Pip.
The star spirit blinked its large gold eyes.
"Star," it said.
Its voice was very small.
"Fell," it added.

Luna pushed forward.
She looked at the star spirit over her glasses.
"I know what these are," said Luna.
"They lose their grip once a year."
"They need to go back before dawn."

They gathered the star spirits.
There were many of them.
Stone held several.
Wren had two in her hat.
They kept bouncing.

"They need height," said Luna.
"The highest point in Moonwood."
Flint reached for his satchel.
Everyone looked at Flint.
"I know exactly where it is," said Flint.
Everyone kept looking at him.

"This way," said Flint.
He was right.
The Tumblerock Cliffs rose ahead.
"You found it," said Pip.
"I always find things," said Flint.
"Eventually."

They climbed to the top.
Moonwood spread below them in the dark.
The sky was full of stars above.
The star spirits reached upward with their tiny arms.

Stone raised their hands high.
Pip and Spark added a little push.
The star spirits lifted.
Up.
And up.

The star spirits went up.
Slowly at first.
Then faster.
One by one they found their places.
The sky filled back up with stars.

The last star spirit sat in Stone's hand.
It blinked its large gold eyes at everyone.
"See you next year," it said.
Then it bounced once.
Then it went up.
Then it was gone.
